title: Inyri Forge
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=GFFA |image=[[File:Inyri_Forge.jpg]] |name=Inyri Forge |homeworld=[[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]]''[[X-Wing: Wedge's Gamble]]'' |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] {{C|[[Kesselian]]}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]] |pronouns=She/her |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Brown]] |feathers= |eyes=Blue |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents=*[[Kassar Forge]] *[[Myda Forge]] |partners= |siblings=[[Lujayne Forge]] |children= |affiliation=*[[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] *[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] **[[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]]''[[X-Wing: The Bacta War]]'' |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I know serving with Rogue Squadron was your sister's dream, but you've earned your own place with us. We'd be proud to have you if you want to stay with us.''"
"''My sister always wanted the best for everyone else. Joining the squadron meant she got to fight the evil plaguing others, making things better for them. Her example is pretty compelling. I'm in.|Wedge Antilles asks Inyri Forge to stay with Rogue Squadron after the Bacta War|}} '''Inyri Forge''' was a [[Human/Legends|human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Kesselian]] from [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]]. IN [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]], when the [[New Republic]] sent [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] to Kessel to [[Mission to Kessel (Galactic Civil War)|release]] criminal members of [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] from [[Prison/Legends|prison]], Forge tagged along with her sweetheart, [[Zekka Thyne]], as he and his fellow rascals were sent to the [[Coruscant Underworld/Legends|Coruscant underworld]] to help the Rogues prepare [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] for a New Republic invasion. Although her sister [[Lujayne Forge|Lujayne]] had recently been [[Death/Legends|killed]] while serving in the Rogues, Inyri eventually softened toward the [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] and helped them prepare the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] for [[Liberation of Coruscant|liberation]] from the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]]. With the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] now in New Republic hands, Forge joined Rogue Squadron, and they all resigned their commissions soon afterward in order to pursue [[Ysanne Isard]] during the [[Bacta War]]. After Isard's [[Battle of Thyferra|defeat]], the Rogues were welcomed back with open arms, and they joined the [[Solo Fleet]] for their next big operation: the [[Hunt for Zsinj|hunt for Warlord Zsinj]]. She also flew in the [[Battle of Bilbringi (9 ABY)|Battle of Bilbringi]] that brought the [[Thrawn campaign]] of [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]] to an end, and in the subsequent [[Hegemony theater|strike]] against the [[Ciutric Hegemony]], where Rogue Squadron teamed up with Isard to take down her [[Ysanne Isard (clone)|clone]]. When the [[Extragalactic/Legends|extra-galactic]] [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invaded]] the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] in [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], Forge was a [[Major/Legends|major]] in Rogue Squadron and the leader of [[Two Flight]]. The Rogues' ranks were [[Starfighter combat|devastated]] by the Yuuzhan Vong's [[Yorik-et|coralskippers]], but Forge survived major battles at [[Battle of Dantooine (Yuuzhan Vong War)|Dantooine]] and [[Battle of Ithor|Ithor]] before the enemy were finally [[Battle of Yuuzhan'tar|defeated]]. In [[43 ABY]], she joined some old friends on a [[Mission to save Kessel|mission]] to save the [[Calrissian-Nunb Mines]] on her [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of Kessel from mysterious [[Groundquake/Legends|groundquakes]]. ==Biography== {{Quote|We gave our daughter—all of our children—all the love and support we could. We trust them. Just as we trusted Lujayne to go off and join the Rebellion, we must trust Inyri.|Kassar Forge, watching his daughter leave Kessel|}} The [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] Inyri Forge was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] on [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]] to [[Kassar Forge|Kassar]] and [[Myda Forge]]. Kassar was a teacher who volunteered to go to Kessel under a [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] program to educate [[Prisoner/Legends|inmates]] on how to re-enter society upon release. Her [[Parent/Legends|mother]] was one of the inmate students who fell in love with him and decided to stay with him on Kessel. Inyri Forge had multiple brothers and sisters, including [[Lujayne Forge|Lujayne]], all of whom were still alive and living on Kessel in [[6 ABY/Legends|6.5 ABY]]. Lujayne became a member of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] reorganized [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] in that [[Standard year/Legends|year]], but she was [[Death/Legends|killed]] by [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtoopers]] during a [[Raid on Talasea|raid]] on the Rogues' [[Talasea base]]. In [[7 ABY]], while the [[New Republic Provisional Council]] was planning a strike at the Imperial [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], they decided to [[Mission to Kessel (Galactic Civil War)|release]] some high profile prisoners in order to cause chaos in the heart of [[Galactic City/Legends|Imperial City]]. [[File:Inyri.jpg|thumb|left|175px|Inyri Forge]] One of those prisoners was [[Zekka Thyne]], of the [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] organization. Along with Zekka came Forge, his "[[Glitterstim/Legends|glitterstim]] [[cutter]]" and lover. Forge, being born on Kessel, and not sent there, was not a criminal, but she was insistent on staying with Thyne, even when the squadron offered to aid her. However, things changed as the undercover mission progressed. Forge began to rethink her loyalty towards Thyne, especially after an incident when they came under fire, and he ran to save himself while Rogue Squadron member [[Gavin Darklighter]] came to her aid. Finally, things came to a head when Thyne attempted to kill the Rogue [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]], whom he blamed for putting him on Kessel in the first place. Forge shot and killed Thyne before he could go through with it. Having proven herself, Forge aided the Rogues during the [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|Liberation of Coruscant]], and she was inducted formally into the squadron in the celebration afterwards. Although she was a [[Politics/Legends|political]] appointee, [[Rogue Leader/Legends|Rogue Leader]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] would have been happy to recruit her anyway. When [[Ysanne Isard]], the leader of the Empire, fled Coruscant and set up base on [[Thyferra/Legends|Thyferra]], the New Republic refused to interfere in the planet's internal politics. Most of Rogue Squadron resigned their commissions in order to go after Isard, and Forge was among their number. [[Antilles's Rogues]] prosecuted the [[Bacta War]] against Isard and ultimately won [[Battle of Thyferra|victory]] over her, and upon their return to Coruscant, the New Republic welcomed them back and informed them that their mission had retroactively been deemed an officially-sanctioned operation. The Rogues next joined the [[Solo Fleet]] in the [[Hunt for Zsinj|hunt for Warlord Zsinj]]; based on the ''[[Mon Remonda]]'', they worked alongside Antilles's new [[Wraith Squadron]] during the [[Standard month/Legends|months]]-long campaign. Their final [[Battle of Selaggis|strike]] on Zsinj's ''[[Iron Fist]]'' [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] dealt a huge blow to the [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]], and [[General/Legends|General]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] was able to land the [[Battle of Dathomir (Hunt for Zsinj)|killing strike]] in [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]]. Forge remained with the Rogues for years, flying against the Empire's [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] in [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]]. The Rogues participated in the [[Battle of Bilbringi (9 ABY)|Battle of Bilbringi]] that saw Thrawn perish and his [[Thrawn's confederation|confederation]] crumble, and immediately afterward, Rogue Squadron were thrust into the [[Hegemony theater|Ciutric campaign]] against Warlord [[Delak Krennel]]. During that operation, she was a member of [[Requiem Squadron]], where she was disguised as "Inyon Fass," who had bright red hair and was the sister of [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Teekon Fass]]. The Rogues found themselves reluctantly teaming up with Ysanne Isard to fight against her [[Ysanne Isard (clone)|clone]], and although she ended up betraying them, the campaign ended with the deaths of both Isard incarnations. Later, Forge achieved the rank of [[Major/Legends|major]], and she continued to serve with the squadron well into the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]]. In [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]] she was a trusted Rogue veteran, and under Rogue Leader Gavin Darklighter, she flew as [[Rogue Five/Legends|Rogue Five]], the leader of [[Two Flight]]. The Rogues's ranks were devastated by the [[Yuuzhan Vong]]'s [[Yorik-et|coralskipper]] [[Starfighter/Legends|fighter craft]], which used their [[dovin basal]] vortices to open up miniature [[Black hole/Legends|black holes]] and absorb their enemies' [[Laser/Legends|laser]] blasts, but Forge and her comrades fought on in the [[Battle of Dantooine (Yuuzhan Vong War)|Battle of Dantooine]] and the [[Battle of Kalarba]]. The invaders were finally [[Battle of Yuuzhan'tar|defeated]] in [[29 ABY/Legends|29 AY]], and Forge, retired by [[43 ABY]], joined a group of friends in that year on a [[Mission to save Kessel|mission]] to save the [[Calrissian-Nunb Mines]] on Kessel from a series of destructive [[Groundquake/Legends|groundquakes]]. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Inyri Forge}} *''[[X-Wing: Wedge's Gamble]]'' {{Ab|X-Wing: Wedge's Gamble (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}} *''[[X-Wing: The Krytos Trap]]'' {{Ab|X-Wing: The Krytos Trap (unabridged audiobook)}} *''[[X-Wing: The Bacta War]]'' {{Ab|X-Wing: The Bacta War (unabridged audiobook)}} *''[[X-Wing: Solo Command]]'' {{Ab|X-Wing: Solo Command (unabridged audiobook)}} *''[[X-Wing: Isard's Revenge]]'' *''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide I: Onslaught]]'' *''[[The New Jedi Order: Balance Point]]'' *''[[Fate of the Jedi: Outcast]]'' {{Ab|Fate of the Jedi: Outcast (audiobook)}} ==Sources== *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *{{InsiderCite|59|Who's Who in Rogue Squadron}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' {{StartBox}} {{SuccessionBox |title=[[Rogue Six/Legends|Rogue Six]] |years=[[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]] |before=[[Riv Shiel]] |after=[[Gavin Darklighter]] }} {{SuccessionBox |title=[[Rogue Twelve]] |years=[[7 ABY/Legends|7.5 ABY]] — |before=[[Aril Nunb]] |after=Eventually [[Anni Capstan]] }} {{SuccessionBox |title=[[Rogue Five/Legends|Rogue Five]] |years=[[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]] |before=Eventually [[Wes Janson/Legends|Wes Janson]] |after=Unknown }} {{EndBox}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |cs=Inyri Forge |de=Inyri Forge }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Forge, Inyri}} [[Category:Antilles's Rogues]] [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Galactic Alliance individuals]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals with she/her pronouns]] [[Category:Kesselians]] [[Category:Rogue Squadron personnel]]
